---
title: "sammy_r21 — scRNA-seq QC"
subtitle: "Pre-filter QC across the cohort, to choose clustering thresholds"
format:
  pptx:
    reference-doc: ouhsc_ppt_template.pptx
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
# | tags: [setup]
from pathlib import Path

import anndata as ad
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import display, Markdown
from matplotlib.lines import Line2D
from scipy.stats import median_abs_deviation

# Every h5ad is staged into this notebook's working directory by the Nextflow
# process, so globbing is the whole input contract — no params to pass or drift.
H5ADS = sorted(Path(".").glob("*.h5ad"))
if not H5ADS:
    raise FileNotFoundError("No .h5ad files staged next to the notebook.")

# Condition palette, carried over from oir-analysis so figures across the two
# projects read the same. Teal/orange is a blue-orange axis, which stays separable
# under the common red-green CVDs.
COLORS = {"normal": "#2AABB8", "obese": "#E87D2A"}

# The reference template is 16:9 at 10 x 5.625in. Every figure is sized to sit
# inside one slide with room for its title — hence one metric per slide rather
# than a tall stacked panel, which only works in a scrolling page.
FIGSIZE = (9.5, 4.5)

METRICS = {
    "n_genes_by_counts": "Genes per cell",
    "total_counts": "UMI counts per cell",
    "pct_counts_mt": "Mitochondrial %",
    "pct_counts_in_top_20_genes": "% counts in top 20 genes",
}
LOG_METRICS = {"n_genes_by_counts", "total_counts"}


def load_obs(path):
    """Read one h5ad's obs without pulling X into memory.

    backed="r" leaves the count matrix on disk; obs is all this report needs, and
    the cohort's X would be ~5 GB.
    """
    adata = ad.read_h5ad(path, backed="r")
    obs = adata.obs.copy()
    adata.file.close()
    return obs


def md_table(df):
    """Render a DataFrame as a markdown table.

    Built by hand rather than via df.style (HTML-only, renders as nothing in pptx)
    or df.to_markdown (needs tabulate, which the container does not carry).
    """
    header = "| " + " | ".join(str(c) for c in df.columns) + " |"
    rule = "| " + " | ".join("---" for _ in df.columns) + " |"
    rows = ["| " + " | ".join(str(v) for v in row) + " |" for row in df.itertuples(index=False)]
    display(Markdown("\n".join([header, rule] + rows)))


def violin_slide(metric, label):
    """One slide: this metric's distribution across every sample, coloured by condition."""
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=FIGSIZE)
    data = [obs.loc[obs["sample"] == s, metric].to_numpy() for s in sample_order]
    parts = ax.violinplot(data, showextrema=False, widths=0.85)
    for body, s in zip(parts["bodies"], sample_order):
        body.set_facecolor(COLORS[sample_condition[s]])
        body.set_alpha(0.75)
        body.set_edgecolor("none")
    # Median as a thin white tick — a violin hides the centre, and the centre is
    # what the threshold conversation is about.
    ax.scatter(range(1, len(data) + 1), [np.median(d) for d in data],
               marker="_", s=90, color="white", zorder=3, linewidths=1.4)

    if metric in LOG_METRICS:
        ax.set_yscale("log")
    ax.set_xticks(range(1, len(sample_order) + 1))
    ax.set_xticklabels(sample_order, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
    ax.set_ylabel(label, fontsize=9)
    ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.25, linewidth=0.5)
    ax.set_axisbelow(True)
    handles = [Line2D([0], [0], marker="s", linestyle="", markersize=8,
                      markerfacecolor=c, markeredgecolor="none", label=k)
               for k, c in COLORS.items()]
    ax.legend(handles=handles, frameon=False, loc="upper right", fontsize=8)
    fig.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


obs = pd.concat([load_obs(p) for p in H5ADS], ignore_index=True)
# Order samples by condition then id so the panels read as two blocks, and every
# figure shares one sample order.
obs["id_num"] = obs["id"].astype(int)
sample_order = (
    obs[["sample", "condition", "id_num"]]
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values(["condition", "id_num"])["sample"]
    .tolist()
)
obs["sample"] = pd.Categorical(obs["sample"], categories=sample_order, ordered=True)
sample_condition = obs.drop_duplicates("sample").set_index("sample")["condition"].to_dict()
n_cells = len(obs)
```

## Cohort

```{python}
lines = [f"**{len(sample_order)} samples · {n_cells:,} cells** (pre-filter)", ""]
for c in COLORS:
    d = obs[obs["condition"] == c]
    lines.append(f"- **{c}**: {d['sample'].nunique()} samples, {len(d):,} cells")
lines += ["", "Every barcode Cell Ranger called is included — `create_adata` filters nothing,",
          "so these are the raw distributions the filtering decision gets made against."]
display(Markdown("\n".join(lines)))
```

## Per-sample summary

```{python}
rows = []
for s in sample_order:
    d = obs[obs["sample"] == s]
    rows.append({
        "sample": s,
        "condition": sample_condition[s],
        "cells": f"{len(d):,}",
        "median genes": f"{round(d['n_genes_by_counts'].median()):,}",
        "median counts": f"{round(d['total_counts'].median()):,}",
        "median mito %": f"{d['pct_counts_mt'].median():.2f}",
    })
md_table(pd.DataFrame(rows))
```

## Genes per cell

```{python}
violin_slide("n_genes_by_counts", "Genes per cell")
```

## UMI counts per cell

```{python}
violin_slide("total_counts", "UMI counts per cell")
```

## Mitochondrial %

```{python}
violin_slide("pct_counts_mt", "Mitochondrial %")
```

## % counts in top 20 genes

```{python}
violin_slide("pct_counts_in_top_20_genes", "% counts in top 20 genes")
```

## Per-sample QC scatter

```{python}
n_cols = 5
n_rows = int(np.ceil(len(sample_order) / n_cols))
fig, axes = plt.subplots(n_rows, n_cols, figsize=FIGSIZE, sharex=True, sharey=True)
axes = np.atleast_1d(axes).ravel()

for ax, s in zip(axes, sample_order):
    d = obs[obs["sample"] == s]
    sc = ax.scatter(
        d["total_counts"], d["n_genes_by_counts"],
        c=d["pct_counts_mt"].clip(upper=25),
        cmap="viridis", vmin=0, vmax=25,
        s=1.0, alpha=0.35, edgecolors="none", rasterized=True,
    )
    ax.set_xscale("log")
    ax.set_yscale("log")
    ax.set_title(f"{s}\n{len(d):,} cells", fontsize=6, color=COLORS[sample_condition[s]])
    ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)
    ax.tick_params(labelsize=5)

for ax in axes[len(sample_order):]:
    ax.set_visible(False)

fig.supxlabel("Total counts", fontsize=8)
fig.supylabel("Genes detected", fontsize=8)
cbar = fig.colorbar(sc, ax=axes.tolist(), fraction=0.02, pad=0.02)
cbar.set_label("Mito % (clipped at 25)", fontsize=6)
cbar.ax.tick_params(labelsize=5)
plt.show()
```

::: {.notes}
Low-count/low-gene points in the bottom-left with high mitochondrial fraction are the
debris population the thresholds are meant to cut. The main manifold is the diagonal.
:::

## Suggested per-sample thresholds (MAD-based)

```{python}
def mad_bounds(values, n_mads, log=False):
    """Return (lower, upper) cutoffs at median ± n_mads · MAD, on the original scale."""
    v = np.log1p(values) if log else np.asarray(values, dtype=float)
    med = np.median(v)
    mad = median_abs_deviation(v)
    lo, hi = med - n_mads * mad, med + n_mads * mad
    return (np.expm1(lo), np.expm1(hi)) if log else (lo, hi)


rows = []
total_drop = 0
for s in sample_order:
    d = obs[obs["sample"] == s]
    genes_lo, genes_hi = mad_bounds(d["n_genes_by_counts"], 5, log=True)
    counts_lo, counts_hi = mad_bounds(d["total_counts"], 5, log=True)
    _mito_lo, mito_hi = mad_bounds(d["pct_counts_mt"], 3)

    drop = (
        (d["n_genes_by_counts"] < genes_lo) | (d["n_genes_by_counts"] > genes_hi)
        | (d["total_counts"] < counts_lo) | (d["total_counts"] > counts_hi)
        | (d["pct_counts_mt"] > mito_hi)
    )
    total_drop += int(drop.sum())
    rows.append({
        "sample": s,
        "min genes": f"{round(genes_lo):,}",
        "max genes": f"{round(genes_hi):,}",
        "min counts": f"{round(counts_lo):,}",
        "max counts": f"{round(counts_hi):,}",
        "max mito %": f"{mito_hi:.1f}",
        "drop": f"{int(drop.sum()):,}",
        "% drop": f"{100 * drop.mean():.1f}",
    })
# Cohort total as a final row, not trailing prose: any text after the table gets
# pushed onto a slide of its own, titled with its first sentence fragment.
rows.append({
    "sample": "cohort", "min genes": "", "max genes": "", "min counts": "",
    "max counts": "", "max mito %": "",
    "drop": f"{total_drop:,}", "% drop": f"{100 * total_drop / n_cells:.1f}",
})
md_table(pd.DataFrame(rows))
```

::: {.notes}
Each sample's cutoffs derive from its own distribution: median ± N·MAD, computed on
log1p for the count and gene metrics (right-skewed, so a symmetric band only makes
sense in log space) and on the raw scale for mitochondrial %. sc-best-practices
formulation: 5 MADs for counts/genes, 3 for mito. A starting point, not an answer —
read against the plots and override per sample where the data disagrees.
:::
